article_title: LG Opens Pre-Orders for Micro RGB evo, Its First Flagship RGB TV, Starting at $4,999
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: LG Opens Pre-Orders for Micro RGB evo, Its First Flagship RGB TV, Starting at $4,999
content_preview.summary: LG's MRGB95 series uses individually controlled RGB LEDs and Intertek-certified Triple 100% Color Coverage across BT.2020, DCI-P3, and Adobe RGB in 75-, 86-, and 100-inch sizes.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

LG Electronics USA opened pre-orders on April 22, 2026 for the Micro RGB evo, a television the company describes as its first flagship RGB TV. The [MRGB95 series](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/lg-announces-pricing-and-availability-for-lg-micro-rgb-evo-lgs-first-flagship-rgb-tv-302749412.html) launches in three sizes — 75, 86, and 100 inches — with prices starting at $4,999.99 for the 75-inch model. The product had its public debut at CES 2026, where the 100-inch model rec...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article covering LG's pre-order launch for its Micro RGB evo TV. Clear organization with distinct sections covering technology, color coverage, pricing, positioning, and unknowns. The '## What We Don't Know' section is a strong editorial touch. Minor prose error: the Color Coverage section contains a doubled phrase ('LG's CES announcement described this as LG described this as achieving...').
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources fetched successfully. source-0.html.gz (PR Newswire pricing release): confirms pre-orders April 22, 2026, $4,999.99 starting price, MRGB95 series, Intertek Triple 100% Color Coverage (BT.2020/DCI-P3/Adobe RGB), Micro Dimming Ultra with 1000+ dimming zones, webOS 26 features — all verified. source-1.html.gz (eCoustics): confirms all three model numbers and prices (75MRGB95BUA $4,999.99, 86MRGB95BUA $6,999.99, 100MRGB95BUA $7,999.99) — verified. source-2.html.gz (LG CES press release): confirms Alpha 11 AI Processor Gen 3, Dual Super Upscaling, Park Hyoung-sei quote verbatim — verified. DOES NOT contain the '5.6 times faster NPU processing compared to its 2025 predecessor' claim attributed to it. source-3.html.gz (Engadget): confirms Micro RGB differs from mini-LED with individual RGB LEDs, OLED contrast advantages, TCL and Samsung also exploring Micro RGB — verified. source-4.html.gz (Gearbrain): confirms 'the Micro RGB evo complements LG's OLED evo lineup rather than replacing it' verbatim, Google Assistant/AirPlay 2/HomeKit/Matter support — verified. FABRICATED SPECIFIC: The claim 'LG describes it as featuring 5.6 times faster NPU processing compared to its 2025 predecessor, per the CES announcement' is not supported by source-2 or any other source. This specific number does not appear anywhere in the 5 snapshots.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core facts (pricing, model numbers, color gamut certifications, launch date, CES debut, Innovation Award) all verified against sources. One fabricated specific identified: the '5.6 times faster NPU' claim is not in any of the cited sources. The CES press release (source-2) describes the processor as 'newly upgraded Dual AI Engine-based Alpha 11 AI Processor Gen 3' without any performance ratio. This unsourced specific is in the body (not headline/summary) and is addressable with a corrections record.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or editorializing. LG's marketing claims are clearly attributed as such. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately flags limitations.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of LG Micro RGB evo found in the archive. New topic.
concerns: The claim 'LG describes it as featuring 5.6 times faster NPU processing compared to its 2025 predecessor' is attributed to the LG CES press release but does not appear in that source (source-2.html.gz) or any other snapshot. This is a fabricated specific that requires a corrections record.
Minor prose error in Color Coverage section: 'LG's CES announcement described this as LG described this as achieving 100 percent...' — doubled phrasing.
recommendations: File a corrections record documenting the unsourced '5.6x faster NPU' claim.
Add ecoustics.com, lg.com, and gearbrain.com to the source allowlist for future submissions (done in this review batch).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively sound article with accurate core facts and good editorial structure. One unsourced performance specific ('5.6 times faster NPU') was introduced without basis in any cited source. Suitable for APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS — the fabricated specific is in the body only and can be honestly documented in a corrections record without gutting the article.
